title: 🎙️ Working Lands & Industry
description: Today's focus is the resource economy that defines the Cariboo — forestry, ranching, mining, and the agricultural technology helping these industries adapt and modernize. We're looking for stories about the people and innovations keeping rural BC's working landscape viable, including tensions between resource extraction and long-term stewardship, and the technology bridging both. - Daily themed article selection for podcast generation

_podcast.theme_description: Today's focus is the resource economy that defines the Cariboo — forestry, ranching, mining, and the agricultural technology helping these industries adapt and modernize. We're looking for stories about the people and innovations keeping rural BC's working landscape viable, including tensions between resource extraction and long-term stewardship, and the technology bridging both.
_podcast.theme_scoring_prompt: Rate how well this article fits the theme 'Working Lands & Industry' — the resource economy that defines the Cariboo and rural BC. Strong fits include: BC and Canadian forestry journalism (mill closures, lumber markets, beetle kill, harvest operations, silviculture, BCTS, old-growth policy), ranching and livestock stories (water rights, grazing management, cattle markets, drought impact on ranches), mining and resource extraction (safety, new operations, Indigenous consultation, supply chains), agriculture in rural Canada (crop insurance, grain prices, farm economics, food sovereignty), and the technology helping these industries adapt — precision forestry, AgTech, drone surveys, IoT in agriculture, mine automation, sawmill modernization, climate adaptation on working lands. Also strong fits: labor and workforce stories in resource industries, environmental stewardship within working landscapes (not pure conservation, but how ranchers/loggers manage for sustainability), and science with direct implications for forestry, agriculture, or extractive industries. Penalise heavily (score 0–20): international geopolitical and war news with no resource-economy connection; consumer tech and gadget reviews; pure wildlife entertainment without agricultural or working-lands relevance; crime and judicial news unrelated to resource industries; financial market and stock ticker stories. Score 0-100 where 100 is a perfect thematic match and 0 is completely unrelated.

items.title: [Boing Boing] Scientists use yeast from body of Ötzi the Iceman to make "very very good" sourdough
items.content_html:
Yeast survived for millenia inside a mummy, and can still be used to make bread.

About 5300 years ago, a Tyrolean man died after being shot in the shoulder with an arrow. His body was so well-preserved in the Alpine ice on the border of Italy and Austria that it was initially believed to be that of a recently deceased hiker. — Read the rest

The post Scientists use yeast from body of Ötzi the Iceman to make "very very good" sourdough appeared first on Boing Boing.
